subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **2** quilt boards (7×7 grid each)
- **20** patch tiles (simpler shapes than original Patchwork)
- **2** time tokens
- **1** central time board
- **Buttons** (currency)
- **1** neutral token (marks starting position in patch circle)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place time board** in the center.
2. **Arrange patches** in a circle around the time board.
3. **Place neutral token** after the smallest patch (2-button cost patch).
4. **Each player** takes a quilt board, time token (placed on start of time board), and 5 buttons.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **5 buttons each** as starting currency.
- **Time tokens on start** of the time track.
- **Quilt board** — Empty 7×7 grid.
- **Player behind on track goes first** (both start equal, so younger player goes first).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Patchwork: Express is a simplified 2-player tile-placement game. Buy patches to fill your quilt board, managing buttons (currency) and time. The player with the most buttons (minus penalties) wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
The player whose time token is further behind on the track takes the turn. On your turn, choose one:

1. **Buy a patch** — Choose one of the 3 patches ahead of the neutral token. Pay its button cost, advance your time token by its time cost, and place it on your quilt board.
2. **Advance** — Move your time token ahead of your opponent's token. Gain 1 button per space moved.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Buying Patches
- Only the 3 patches immediately clockwise of the neutral token are available.
- Pay the button cost shown on the patch.
- Advance your time token by the time cost shown.
- Place the patch on your quilt board (may rotate/flip but cannot overlap).
- Move the neutral token to where the purchased patch was.

#### Button Income
- When your time token passes a button icon on the time track, earn buttons equal to the button icons shown on patches already on your quilt board.

#### Special Patches
- Single-square patches on the time board — when you pass one first, take it and place on your quilt.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Patches are simpler** — Express uses fewer and simpler patch shapes than standard Patchwork.
- **7×7 bonus** — First player to fill a 7×7 area on their quilt gets a bonus token (varies by edition).
- **Cannot rearrange** — Once placed, patches cannot be moved.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- Game ends when both time tokens reach the end of the time track.

#### Scoring
- Count your buttons (currency remaining).
- Subtract 2 points for each empty space on your quilt board.
- Highest score wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Fill your board** — Empty spaces cost 2 points each; prioritize coverage.
- **Button income** — Buy patches with button icons early for recurring income.
- **Time management** — Big patches advance your time a lot; balance cost vs coverage.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Income engine** — Early button-generating patches create long-term income.
- **Deny key patches** — If your opponent needs a specific shape, consider buying it first.
- **Advance for income** — Sometimes advancing past button income icons is more valuable than buying a patch.
- **Corner filling** — Prioritize filling corners and edges that are hard to fill later.
